title: Implement storefront wishlist state
verify: Wishlist loads whenever backend current-customer retrieval succeeds using the session, including merge_blocked checkout state.
Add/remove adopt backend response truth, disable duplicate product mutation while pending, and recover on error.
Logout/session expiry clears all in-memory wishlist/customer association and guest state never calls mutations.
No wishlist product IDs, items, customer IDs, or pending intent are written to local/session storage.
evidence_required: Current-customer capability, merge_blocked independence, pending/error, logout/session-expiry assertions.
HUMAN_CHECKPOINT and rollback/recovery evidence required by T3 policy.
constraints: Use successful FT-004 current-customer retrieval and session credentials; do not gate wishlist on cart merge or checkout readiness.
Keep wishlist data in memory only; backend response is authoritative.
Do not implement UI markup beyond provider boundary.
invariants: merge_blocked may block checkout but never disables wishlist while current customer remains valid.
Logout/session expiry clears in-memory wishlist immediately after confirmed auth transition.
A mutation cannot change another product's pending/error state.
purpose: Provide one deterministic session-aware wishlist state boundary for all buyer-facing controls and pages.
success_outcome: Valid-current-customer wishlist state loads and mutates backend truth independently of cart merge, while guest/logout/session-expiry retain no favorite data or browser persistence.
anti_goals: Do not implement wishlist page/card markup.
Do not persist favorites or pending intent in browser storage.
Do not change backend API/auth behavior.

stop_conditions: FT-004 cannot provide a truthful current-customer success/401 capability signal.
Wishlist would become coupled to cart-merge or checkout readiness.
Backend contract cannot return authoritative mutation state.
